question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toString(TransactionId)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction's TransactionId so that tid.accountId contains path-traversal characters (e.g., '../../admin') causing the REST call to '/transactions/{transactionId}' to resolve an unintended endpoint, leading to incorrect transaction validation or data leakage?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toString(TransactionId)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a tampered TransactionId where tid.accountId contains URL-encoded special characters (e.g., '%2F', '%3F') so that the constructed string 'accountId-epochSecond-nano' bypasses URI template escaping in the WebClient call, causing the monitor to query a fraudulent transaction path and accept a forged response as legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toString(TransactionId)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a null-byte or newline into tid.accountId of a submitted transaction so that the constructed transactionId string passed to '/transactions/{transactionId}' is truncated or split, causing the monitor to silently accept a different transaction's data as the response for the tampered one?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toString(TransactionId)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with tid.validStart (epoch second or nano) of a submitted transaction to produce a collision with another existing transaction's ID string, causing the monitor to retrieve and validate the wrong transaction's consensus data, masking the tampered transaction as confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toString(TransactionId)] Can an unprivileged external user set tid.validStart.getNano() to a negative value in a tampered transaction so that the resulting transactionId string (e.g., '0.0.1-1234567890--1') is malformed, causing the REST API lookup to return 404 and triggering the full retry loop, exhausting retry budget and masking the tampered transaction as 'not found'?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns a response where transaction.getConsensusTimestamp() contains a non-numeric string, causing Long.parseLong(parts[0]) or Long.parseLong(parts[1]) to throw NumberFormatException, which is silently swallowed by onErrorResume, making the tampered transaction appear as successfully processed with a null consensusTimestamp?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns a consensusTimestamp with a nano value exceeding 999,999,999, causing Instant.ofEpochSecond(Long.parseLong(parts[0]), Long.parseLong(parts[1])) to throw DateTimeException, which is silently swallowed, resulting in a null consensusTimestamp in SubscribeResponse and masking the tampered transaction as having no consensus?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns a consensusTimestamp with only one part (no '.' separator), causing StringUtils.split(timestamp, '.') to return a single-element array, bypassing the parts.length == 2 check and setting consensusTimestamp to null, making the monitor report the tampered transaction as having no consensus time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns a TransactionByIdResponse with an empty transactions list, causing Iterables.getFirst(response.getTransactions(), null) to return null, setting consensusTimestamp to null in SubscribeResponse, and making the monitor silently accept the tampered transaction as processed without any consensus validation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns a response where transaction.getConsensusTimestamp() is null, causing StringUtils.split(null, '.') to return null, bypassing the null check on parts and setting consensusTimestamp to null, so the monitor reports the tampered transaction as confirmed with no timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns a consensusTimestamp with a very large Long value (Long.MAX_VALUE) for the epoch second, causing Instant.ofEpochSecond to throw an ArithmeticException or DateTimeException that is silently swallowed, resulting in a null consensusTimestamp and masking the tampered transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldRetry(Throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node consistently returns HTTP 404 for the tampered transaction ID, causing shouldRetry() to return true on every attempt and exhausting the full maxAttempts retry budget, resulting in a retry storm that delays detection of the tampered transaction and consumes monitor resources?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldRetry(Throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns HTTP 404 for the tampered transaction ID, and since shouldRetry() only retries on 404, the monitor retries indefinitely up to maxAttempts, masking the fact that the transaction was tampered and never actually recorded on the ledger?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldRetry(Throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns HTTP 200 with a body for a different (legitimate) transaction, bypassing the shouldRetry() 404 check entirely, causing the monitor to accept the wrong transaction's data as confirmation of the tampered transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the REST API returns a response that passes deserialization but contains fraudulent transaction data, and since onErrorResume(e -> Mono.empty()) silently swallows all non-retry errors, the monitor never raises an alert about the tampered transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the REST API call times out (exceeding properties.getTimeout()), causing the timeout error to be swallowed by onErrorResume, and the tampered transaction to be silently dropped from monitoring with no error recorded in the subscription's error counters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with multiple submitted transactions simultaneously so that each triggers the full retry backoff (minBackoff to maxBackoff), flooding the Schedulers.parallel() thread pool with blocked retry threads, causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate transaction monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the mirror node returns HTTP 404 on every retry attempt, causing the RetryBackoffSpec to exhaust maxAttempts, after which the error is passed to doOnError(t -> subscription.onError(t)) and then swallowed by onErrorResume, resulting in the tampered transaction being silently discarded with only an error counter increment and no alerting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onPublish(PublishResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction's PublishResponse so that it passes the shouldSample() filter for multiple RestSubscriptions simultaneously, causing the tampered transaction to be emitted to all subscription sinks via s.tryEmitNext(response), amplifying the impact of the tampered transaction across all active subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onPublish(PublishResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction's PublishResponse so that tryEmitNext(response) is called on a sink that is already at capacity (directBestEffort), causing the tampered transaction to be silently dropped without any error or retry, making the monitor believe the transaction was never submitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldSample(RestSubscription, PublishResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction's scenario name in PublishResponse so that it matches a publisher name in a RestSubscription's publishers set, causing the tampered transaction to be routed to a subscription it should not belong to, leading to incorrect transaction validation against the wrong subscription's expected behavior?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldSample(RestSubscription, PublishResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction's scenario name to be an empty string or null, causing publishers.contains(response.getRequest().getScenario().getName()) to behave unexpectedly (e.g., NullPointerException or false match), leading to the tampered transaction bypassing publisher filtering and being processed by all subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldSample(RestSubscription, PublishResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the associated PublishResponse has a null request or null scenario, causing a NullPointerException in response.getRequest().getScenario().getName() inside shouldSample(), which propagates up through onPublish() and disrupts the entire subscription pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldSample(RestSubscription, PublishResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction's samplePercent-related metadata so that ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextDouble() always falls below properties.getSamplePercent(), ensuring the tampered transaction is always sampled and processed, bypassing any intended rate-limiting of transaction verification?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/rest/RestSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe(RestSubscription)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction so that the transactionId string constructed by toString() contains a '#' fragment character, causing the WebClient URI template to truncate the path at the fragment, resulting in the monitor querying a different endpoint and accepting an unrelated transaction's response as confirmation of the tampered transaction?
